_comment: Engram hooks fragment. ONE cross-platform SessionStart entry, grafted into the target's .claude/settings.json by both installers: shell 'bash' runs via Git Bash on Windows (ships with Git for Windows, which Engram requires anyway) and native bash on macOS/Linux, so the committed settings.json works for the whole team. PowerShell-only fallback (no Git Bash): replace command with  powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "$env:CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/engram-brief.ps1"  and shell with 'powershell'. Note: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} is expanded by the hook's shell from the environment - PowerShell syntax needs $env:, bash needs $ or ${}. The '_autocapture_hooks' key below is OPT-IN documentation/data for the installer ONLY - it is NOT part of the default merge. Re-run the installer with -AutoCapture / --auto-capture to graft those two entries into hooks.PreCompact and hooks.SessionEnd.

_autocapture_hooks._comment: OPT-IN, NOT merged by default. The installer grafts these two entries into hooks.PreCompact and hooks.SessionEnd only when run with -AutoCapture / --auto-capture. Both draft a journal entry from the session transcript via headless Claude (engram-capture). PreCompact is synchronous (timeout 180) so compaction waits for the draft - that is the point. SessionEnd is async with a longer timeout (300) so it never blocks CLI exit.
